message.content: Count the total lines of Go code in core/pkg/tailer/. Report the count.

content.text: I'll help you count the total lines of Go code in the core/pkg/tailer/ directory.
Now I'll read each file and count the total lines:

content.content:
1	package tailer
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"irrlicht/core/pkg/capacity"
14	)
15	
16	// MessageEvent represents a single message event from transcript
17	type MessageEvent struct {
18		Timestamp time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
19		EventType string    `json:"event_type"`
20		Content   string    `json:"content,omitempty"`
21	}
22	
23	// SessionMetrics holds computed performance metrics
24	type SessionMetrics struct {
25		MessagesPerMinute   float64        `json:"messages_per_minute"`
26		ElapsedSeconds      int64          `json:"elapsed_seconds"`
27		LastMessageAt       time.Time      `json:"last_message_at"`
28		MessageHistory      []MessageEvent `json:"-"` // Sliding window, not serialized
29		SessionStartAt      time.Time      `json:"session_start_at"`
30		TotalTokens         int64          `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
31		InputTokens         int64          `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
32		OutputTokens        int64          `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
33		CacheReadTokens     int64          `json:"cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
34		CacheCreationTokens int64          `json:"cache_creation_tokens,omitempty"`
35		// Cumulative token totals across all API turns (for cost calculation).
36		CumInputTokens         int64   `json:"cum_input_tokens,omitempty"`
37		CumOutputTokens        int64   `json:"cum_output_tokens,omitempty"`
38		CumCacheReadTokens     int64   `json:"cum_cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
39		CumCacheCreationTokens int64   `json:"cum_cache_creation_tokens,omitempty"`
40		EstimatedCostUSD    float64        `json:"estimated_cost_usd,omitempty"`
41		ModelName           string         `json:"model_name,omitempty"`
42		ContextWindow       int64          `json:"context_window,omitempty"`
43		ContextUtilization  float64        `json:"context_utilization_percentage,omitempty"`
44		PressureLevel       string         `json:"pressure_level,omitempty"` // "safe", "caution", "warning", "critical"
45	
46		// Raw event data for real-time client-side calculations
47		TotalEventCount        int64     `json:"total_event_count,omitempty"`
48		RecentEventCount       int64     `json:"recent_event_count,omitempty"`
49		RecentEventWindowStart time.Time `json:"recent_event_window_start,omitempty"`
50	
51		// Tool call tracking — count unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs
52		HasOpenToolCall   bool `json:"has_open_tool_call"`
53		OpenToolCallCount int  `json:"open_tool_call_count,omitempty"`
54	
55		// OpenSubagents is the number of in-process child agents currently
56		// running. The tailer leaves this at zero; adapters populate it from
57		// LastOpenToolNames or whatever adapter-specific signal they use.
58		OpenSubagents int `json:"open_subagents,omitempty"`
59	
60		// LastEventType is the event type of the most recent message event in
61		// the transcript (e.g. "assistant", "user", "tool_use", "tool_result").
62		// Used for content-based working/waiting detection.
63		LastEventType string `json:"last_event_type,omitempty"`
64	
65		// LastOpenToolNames holds the tool names from the most recent assistant
66		// message that called tools. Cleared when a user message appears.
67		// Used to detect user-blocking tools (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode).
68		LastOpenToolNames []string `json:"last_open_tool_names,omitempty"`
69	
70		// LastWasUserInterrupt is true when the most recent user event was a
71		// real ESC cancellation (the exact "[Request interrupted by user]" text
72		// marker, without the "for tool use" suffix). Reset when any subsequent
73		// non-interrupt user event arrives. The classifier uses this to
74		// transition working/waiting → ready on genuine interrupts without
75		// being fooled by normal tool failures or tool denials.
76		LastWasUserInterrupt bool `json:"last_was_user_interrupt"`
77	
78		// LastWasToolDenial is true when the most recent user event was a tool
79		// denial — the user clicked "no" on a permission prompt, producing the
80		// "[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" text marker. Distinct
81		// from LastWasUserInterrupt because a denial does NOT end the agent's
82		// turn (the agent typically continues with a different approach), so
83		// it must not feed the cancellation rule. Surfaced for observability
84		// and replay-harness flicker categorization.
85		LastWasToolDenial bool `json:"last_was_tool_denial,omitempty"`
86	
87		// LastCWD is the most recent working directory seen in the transcript.
88		// Extracted during parsing so callers don't need a separate file read.
89		LastCWD string `json:"last_cwd,omitempty"`
90	
91		// LastAssistantText is the text content of the most recent assistant
92		// message, truncated to ~200 characters.
93		LastAssistantText string `json:"last_assistant_text,omitempty"`
94	
95		// PermissionMode is the session's permission mode (e.g. "default",
96		// "plan", "bypassPermissions"). Extracted from "permission-mode" events.
97		PermissionMode string `json:"permission_mode,omitempty"`
98	}
99	
100	// TranscriptTailer monitors transcript files and computes metrics.
101	// Format-specific parsing is delegated to a TranscriptParser.
102	type TranscriptTailer struct {
103		path        string
104		lastOffset  int64
105		metrics     *SessionMetrics
106		windowSize  time.Duration // Default 60 seconds
107		capacityMgr *capacity.CapacityManager
108	
109		// parser handles format-specific line parsing (Claude Code, Codex, Pi).
110		parser TranscriptParser
111	
112		// adapter name for model config fallback.
113		adapter string
114	
115		// Context window override from transcript or extended context model suffix.
116		contextWindowOverride int64
117	
118		// openToolCalls is the single source of truth for currently-open tool
119		// calls. Keyed by tool call ID; value is the tool name. Tool uses
120		// insert by ID (idempotent — duplicate IDs overwrite), tool results
121		// delete by ID (orphan IDs are harmless no-ops). HasOpenToolCall and
122		// OpenToolCallCount are derived from len(openToolCalls).
123		//
124		// Historical note: this was originally paired integer counters
125		// (toolUseCount/toolResultCount, see #102), then a []string FIFO
126		// (lastOpenToolNames, see #114). Both had the same structural weakness:
127		// no correlation between a tool_result and the tool_use it pertains to.
128		// The id-keyed map eliminates phantom entries from orphan results,
129		// duplicate tool_use events (multi-line splits), and out-of-order
130		// parallel tool closures. See issue #117.
131		openToolCalls map[string]string
132	
133		// contentChars accumulates character count from message content for
134		// token estimation when explicit token counts aren't available.
135		contentChars int64
136	
137		// Token breakdown accumulators (latest snapshot, not cumulative).
138		// Used for context utilization — always reflects the most recent API turn.
139		inputTokens         int64
140		outputTokens        int64
141		cacheReadTokens     int64
142		cacheCreationTokens int64
143	
144		// Cumulative token accumulators for cost calculation.
145		// These sum the FINAL usage from each unique API turn (requestId).
146		cumInputTokens         int64
147		cumOutputTokens        int64
148		cumCacheReadTokens     int64
149		cumCacheCreationTokens int64
150	
151		// Deduplication: track requestId to avoid double-counting streaming events
152		// within a single API turn. When the requestId changes, the previous turn's
153		// final snapshot is flushed to the cumulative accumulators.
154		lastRequestID   string
155		pendingSnapshot *TokenSnapshot // latest snapshot for current requestId; flushed on ID change
156	
157		// lastWasUserInterrupt tracks whether the most recent user event was
158		// an ESC cancellation (the exact "[Request interrupted by user]" text
159		// marker — NOT the "for tool use" suffix variant).
160		lastWasUserInterrupt bool
161	
162		// lastWasToolDenial tracks whether the most recent user event was a
163		// tool denial ("[Request interrupted by user for tool use]" marker).
164		// Kept distinct from lastWasUserInterrupt so the cancellation rule
165		// only fires on real ESC, not on denials (which don't end the turn).
166		lastWasToolDenial bool
167	
168		// lastCWD tracks the most recent working directory seen in transcript lines.
169		lastCWD string
170	
171		// lastAssistantText holds the text content of the most recent assistant
172		// message, truncated to ~200 characters.
173		lastAssistantText string
174	}
175	
176	// NewTranscriptTailer creates a new tailer for the given transcript path.
177	// The parser handles format-specific line parsing; adapter is used for model
178	// config fallback.
179	func NewTranscriptTailer(path string, parser TranscriptParser, adapter string) *TranscriptTailer {
180		return &TranscriptTailer{
181			path:          path,
182			lastOffset:    0,
183			capacityMgr:   capacity.DefaultCapacityManager(),
184			parser:        parser,
185			adapter:       adapter,
186			openToolCalls: make(map[string]string),
187			metrics: &SessionMetrics{
188				MessageHistory: make([]MessageEvent, 0),
189				SessionStartAt: time.Time{},
190			},
191			windowSize: 60 * time.Second,
192		}
193	}
194	
195	// SetSessionStartTime allows preserving the session start time across multiple invocations
196	func (t *TranscriptTailer) SetSessionStartTime(startTime time.Time) {
197		if t.metrics != nil {
198			t.metrics.SessionStartAt = startTime
199		}
200	}
201	
202	// TailAndProcess reads the last ~64KB of transcript and processes new entries
203	func (t *TranscriptTailer) TailAndProcess() (*SessionMetrics, error) {
204		file, err := os.Open(t.path)
205		if err != nil {
206			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open transcript: %w", err)
207		}
208		defer file.Close()
209	
210		stat, err := file.Stat()
211		if err != nil {
212			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to stat transcript: %w", err)
213		}
214		fileSize := stat.Size()
215	
216		const maxTailSize = 64 * 1024
217		startPos := int64(0)
218		switch {
219		case fileSize < t.lastOffset:
220			// File rotated/truncated — reset cumulative accumulators to avoid
221			// double-counting tokens from the previous file.
222			startPos = 0
223			t.cumInputTokens = 0
224			t.cumOutputTokens = 0
225			t.cumCacheReadTokens = 0
226			t.cumCacheCreationTokens = 0
227			t.lastRequestID = ""
228			t.pendingSnapshot = nil
229		case t.lastOffset > 0:
230			// Normal incremental path: never skip ahead of the last processed byte.
231			startPos = t.lastOffset
232		case fileSize > maxTailSize:
233			// Initial read for large files: only tail the latest window.
234			startPos = fileSize - maxTailSize
235		}
236	
237		_, err = file.Seek(startPos, io.SeekStart)
238		if err != nil {
239			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to seek transcript: %w", err)
240		}
241	
242		currentOffset := startPos
243		var reader io.Reader = file
244	
245		// On the initial truncated read of a large file, we may start in the
246		// middle of a JSON line. If so, discard the partial line to align scanner
247		// to a full JSONL entry boundary.
248		if t.lastOffset == 0 && startPos > 0 {
249			prev := []byte{0}
250			if _, err := file.ReadAt(prev, startPos-1); err == nil && prev[0] != '\n' {
251				br := bufio.NewReader(file)
252				if discarded, err := br.ReadString('\n'); err == nil {
253					currentOffset += int64(len(discarded))
254				} else {
255					return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to align transcript boundary: %w", err)
256				}
257				reader = br
258			}
259		}
260	
261		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(reader)
262		// Large tool results (especially from Pi/Codex read/bash output) can exceed
263		// bufio.Scanner's 64KB default token size.
264		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 2*1024*1024)
265	
266		for scanner.Scan() {
267			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
268			currentOffset += int64(len(scanner.Bytes()) + 1)
269	
270			if line == "" {
271				continue
272			}
273	
274			// Quick JSON check.
275			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "{") || !strings.HasSuffix(line, "}") {
276				continue
277			}
278	
279			var raw map[string]interface{}
280			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &raw); err != nil {
281				continue
282			}
283	
284			// Delegate to format-specific parser.
285			parsed := t.parser.ParseLine(raw)
286			if parsed == nil || parsed.Skip {
287				// Even for skipped events, apply metadata that the parser extracted
288				// (e.g. model from model_change, CWD from session header).
289				if parsed != nil {
290					t.applyMetadata(parsed)
291				}
292				continue
293			}
294	
295			// Apply tool tracking deltas from the parser. openToolCalls is an
296			// id-keyed map — tool_use events insert by ID (idempotent: duplicate
297			// IDs from multi-line splits overwrite), tool_result events delete by
298			// ID (orphan IDs with no matching entry are harmless no-ops). This
299			// eliminates the FIFO's structural weakness where out-of-order or
300			// orphan results could pop unrelated entries. See issue #117.
301			for _, tu := range parsed.ToolUses {
302				if tu.ID != "" {
303					t.openToolCalls[tu.ID] = tu.Name
304				}
305			}
306			for _, id := range parsed.ToolResultIDs {
307				delete(t.openToolCalls, id)
308			}
309			if parsed.ClearToolNames && len(parsed.ToolResultIDs) == 0 {
310				t.openToolCalls = make(map[string]string)
311			}
312			// turn_done is Claude Code's authoritative end-of-turn signal. By
313			// definition every non-Agent tool_use opened during the turn has
314			// already received its tool_result, so anything still in
315			// openToolCalls is a stale leak. Sweeping here lets the classifier
316			// see HasOpenToolCall=false and transition working → ready.
317			//
318			// Agent tool calls are preserved: a sub-agent spawned via the Agent
319			// tool can still be running when the parent's turn_done fires
320			// (session.go:IsAgentDone treats open tool calls as authoritative
321			// over turn_done for exactly this reason), and the claudecode
322			// adapter's CountOpenSubagents relies on Agent entries in
323			// LastOpenToolNames to count in-process sub-agents. Only non-Agent
324			// leaks are swept.
325			if parsed.EventType == "turn_done" && len(t.openToolCalls) > 0 {
326				for id, name := range t.openToolCalls {
327					if name != "Agent" {
328						delete(t.openToolCalls, id)
329					}
330				}
331			}
332			// IsUserInterrupt and IsToolDenial each set their own sticky flag;
333			// any subsequent user event that isn't itself the same kind clears
334			// it. The two flags are tracked independently because only ESC
335			// feeds the classifier's cancellation rule — denials are recorded
336			// for observability but don't end the agent's turn.
337			// parsed.IsError is for tool_result errors — not used by the
338			// classifier, so we don't track it.
339			if parsed.IsUserInterrupt {
340				t.lastWasUserInterrupt = true
341			} else if IsUserEventType(parsed.EventType) {
342				t.lastWasUserInterrupt = false
343			}
344			if parsed.IsToolDenial {
345				t.lastWasToolDenial = true
346			} else if IsUserEventType(parsed.EventType) {
347				t.lastWasToolDenial = false
348			}
349	
350			// Apply metadata.
351			t.applyMetadata(parsed)
352	
353			// Track assistant text.
354			if parsed.AssistantText != "" {
355				t.lastAssistantText = parsed.AssistantText
356			}
357			if parsed.ClearToolNames {
358				t.lastAssistantText = ""
359			}
360	
361			// Accumulate content chars.
362			t.contentChars += parsed.ContentChars
363	
364			t.addMessageEvent(MessageEvent{
365				Timestamp: parsed.Timestamp,
366				EventType: parsed.EventType,
367			})
368		}
369	
370		t.lastOffset = currentOffset
371	
372		// Compute current metrics.
373		t.computeMetrics()
374	
375		// Model config fallback.
376		if t.metrics.ModelName == "" {
377			if defaultModel := getDefaultModelFromConfig(t.adapter); defaultModel != "" {
378				t.metrics.ModelName = defaultModel
379			}
380		}
381	
382		// Estimate tokens from content chars when no explicit token data exists.
383		if t.metrics.TotalTokens == 0 && t.contentChars > 0 && t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
384			cap := t.capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(t.metrics.ModelName)
385			ratio := cap.CharToTokenRatio
386			if ratio <= 0 {
387				ratio = 4.0
388			}
389			t.metrics.TotalTokens = int64(float64(t.contentChars) / ratio)
390			if t.inputTokens == 0 && t.outputTokens == 0 {
391				t.inputTokens = t.metrics.TotalTokens * 9 / 10
392				t.outputTokens = t.metrics.TotalTokens - t.inputTokens
393			}
394		}
395	
396		// Recompute cost if token estimation filled in the breakdown but no
397		// cumulative data is available yet (char-based fallback only).
398		if t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD == 0 && t.inputTokens > 0 &&
399			t.cumInputTokens == 0 && t.cumOutputTokens == 0 && t.pendingSnapshot == nil &&
400			t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
401			t.metrics.InputTokens = t.inputTokens
402			t.metrics.OutputTokens = t.outputTokens
403			t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD = t.capacityMgr.EstimateCostUSD(
404				t.metrics.ModelName, t.inputTokens, t.outputTokens, t.cacheReadTokens, t.cacheCreationTokens)
405		}
406	
407		t.computeContextUtilization()
408	
409		return t.metrics, scanner.Err()
410	}
411	
412	// applyMetadata applies model/token/CWD/permission metadata from a parsed event.
413	func (t *TranscriptTailer) applyMetadata(parsed *ParsedEvent) {
414		if parsed.ModelName != "" {
415			if strings.Contains(parsed.ModelName, "[1m]") {
416				t.contextWindowOverride = 1000000
417			}
418			t.metrics.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(parsed.ModelName)
419		}
420		if parsed.ContextWindow > 0 {
421			t.contextWindowOverride = parsed.ContextWindow
422		}
423		if parsed.Tokens != nil {
424			if parsed.Tokens.Total > 0 {
425				t.metrics.TotalTokens = parsed.Tokens.Total
426			}
427			// Snapshot fields — always overwritten with latest turn for context utilization.
428			if parsed.Tokens.Input > 0 || parsed.Tokens.Output > 0 {
429				t.inputTokens = parsed.Tokens.Input
430				t.outputTokens = parsed.Tokens.Output
431				t.cacheReadTokens = parsed.Tokens.CacheRead
432				t.cacheCreationTokens = parsed.Tokens.CacheCreation
433			}
434		}
435	
436		// Cumulative token accumulation for cost calculation.
437		if parsed.CumulativeTokens != nil {
438			// Codex-style: authoritative cumulative total provided directly.
439			t.cumInputTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.Input
440			t.cumOutputTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.Output
441			t.cumCacheReadTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.CacheRead
442			t.cumCacheCreationTokens = parsed.CumulativeTokens.CacheCreation
443			t.pendingSnapshot = nil
444		} else if parsed.Tokens != nil && parsed.RequestID != "" {
445			// Claude Code-style: deduplicate by requestId — multiple streaming
446			// events share the same requestId within one API turn; only the final
447			// event's tokens should be counted.
448			if parsed.RequestID != t.lastRequestID && t.lastRequestID != "" && t.pendingSnapshot != nil {
449				// Flush previous turn's final snapshot to accumulators.
450				t.cumInputTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.Input
451				t.cumOutputTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.Output
452				t.cumCacheReadTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheRead
453				t.cumCacheCreationTokens += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheCreation
454			}
455			t.pendingSnapshot = parsed.Tokens
456			t.lastRequestID = parsed.RequestID
457		} else if parsed.Tokens != nil && (parsed.Tokens.Input > 0 || parsed.Tokens.Output > 0) {
458			// No requestId (Pi-style): accumulate directly, no dedup needed.
459			t.cumInputTokens += parsed.Tokens.Input
460			t.cumOutputTokens += parsed.Tokens.Output
461			t.cumCacheReadTokens += parsed.Tokens.CacheRead
462			t.cumCacheCreationTokens += parsed.Tokens.CacheCreation
463		}
464		if parsed.CWD != "" {
465			t.lastCWD = parsed.CWD
466		}
467		if parsed.PermissionMode != "" {
468			t.metrics.PermissionMode = parsed.PermissionMode
469		}
470	}
471	
472	// addMessageEvent adds a new message event and maintains sliding window.
473	// Tool call counting is NOT done here — it's handled from ParsedEvent deltas
474	// in TailAndProcess to avoid double-counting.
475	func (t *TranscriptTailer) addMessageEvent(event MessageEvent) {
476		t.metrics.MessageHistory = append(t.metrics.MessageHistory, event)
477		t.metrics.LastMessageAt = event.Timestamp
478		t.metrics.LastEventType = event.EventType
479	
480		if t.metrics.SessionStartAt.IsZero() || event.Timestamp.Before(t.metrics.SessionStartAt) {
481			t.metrics.SessionStartAt = event.Timestamp
482		}
483	}
484	
485	// computeMetrics calculates messages per minute and elapsed time
486	func (t *TranscriptTailer) computeMetrics() {
487		if len(t.metrics.MessageHistory) == 0 {
488			t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = 0
489			t.metrics.ElapsedSeconds = 0
490			t.metrics.TotalEventCount = 0
491			t.metrics.RecentEventCount = 0
492			t.metrics.RecentEventWindowStart = time.Time{}
493			return
494		}
495	
496		currentTime := time.Now()
497		latestTime := t.metrics.LastMessageAt
498		if latestTime.IsZero() {
499			latestTime = currentTime
500		}
501	
502		if !t.metrics.SessionStartAt.IsZero() {
503			t.metrics.ElapsedSeconds = int64(latestTime.Sub(t.metrics.SessionStartAt).Seconds())
504		}
505	
506		t.metrics.TotalEventCount = int64(len(t.metrics.MessageHistory))
507	
508		fiveMinutesAgo := currentTime.Add(-5 * time.Minute)
509		windowStart := fiveMinutesAgo
510		if t.metrics.SessionStartAt.After(fiveMinutesAgo) {
511			windowStart = t.metrics.SessionStartAt
512		}
513		t.metrics.RecentEventWindowStart = windowStart
514	
515		recentEventCount := int64(0)
516		for _, msg := range t.metrics.MessageHistory {
517			if msg.Timestamp.After(windowStart) || msg.Timestamp.Equal(windowStart) {
518				recentEventCount++
519			}
520		}
521		t.metrics.RecentEventCount = recentEventCount
522	
523		// Open tool calls are derived directly from the id-keyed map — the only
524		// source of truth. See the openToolCalls field comment for history (#102,
525		// #114, #117).
526		openCalls := len(t.openToolCalls)
527		t.metrics.OpenToolCallCount = openCalls
528		t.metrics.HasOpenToolCall = openCalls > 0
529		names := make([]string, 0, openCalls)
530		for _, name := range t.openToolCalls {
531			names = append(names, name)
532		}
533		t.metrics.LastOpenToolNames = names
534		t.metrics.LastWasUserInterrupt = t.lastWasUserInterrupt
535		t.metrics.LastWasToolDenial = t.lastWasToolDenial
536		t.metrics.LastCWD = t.lastCWD
537		t.metrics.LastAssistantText = t.lastAssistantText
538	
539		// Token snapshot (latest turn — for context utilization display).
540		t.metrics.InputTokens = t.inputTokens
541		t.metrics.OutputTokens = t.outputTokens
542		t.metrics.CacheReadTokens = t.cacheReadTokens
543		t.metrics.CacheCreationTokens = t.cacheCreationTokens
544	
545		// Cumulative tokens (sum of all turns — for cost calculation).
546		// Include the unflushed pendingSnapshot from the current/final requestId.
547		effectiveCumInput := t.cumInputTokens
548		effectiveCumOutput := t.cumOutputTokens
549		effectiveCumCacheRead := t.cumCacheReadTokens
550		effectiveCumCacheCreate := t.cumCacheCreationTokens
551		if t.pendingSnapshot != nil {
552			effectiveCumInput += t.pendingSnapshot.Input
553			effectiveCumOutput += t.pendingSnapshot.Output
554			effectiveCumCacheRead += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheRead
555			effectiveCumCacheCreate += t.pendingSnapshot.CacheCreation
556		}
557		t.metrics.CumInputTokens = effectiveCumInput
558		t.metrics.CumOutputTokens = effectiveCumOutput
559		t.metrics.CumCacheReadTokens = effectiveCumCacheRead
560		t.metrics.CumCacheCreationTokens = effectiveCumCacheCreate
561	
562		if t.capacityMgr != nil && t.metrics.ModelName != "" {
563			t.metrics.EstimatedCostUSD = t.capacityMgr.EstimateCostUSD(
564				t.metrics.ModelName, effectiveCumInput, effectiveCumOutput,
565				effectiveCumCacheRead, effectiveCumCacheCreate)
566		}
567	
568		// Sliding window for messages per minute.
569		legacyWindowStart := latestTime.Add(-t.windowSize)
570		messageCount := 0
571		filteredHistory := make([]MessageEvent, 0, len(t.metrics.MessageHistory))
572		for _, msg := range t.metrics.MessageHistory {
573			if msg.Timestamp.After(legacyWindowStart) || msg.Timestamp.Equal(legacyWindowStart) {
574				filteredHistory = append(filteredHistory, msg)
575				messageCount++
576			}
577		}
578		t.metrics.MessageHistory = filteredHistory
579	
580		if messageCount > 0 {
581			if len(filteredHistory) > 1 {
582				timeSpan := latestTime.Sub(filteredHistory[0].Timestamp)
583				if timeSpan > 0 {
584					t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount) / timeSpan.Minutes()
585				} else {
586					t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount)
587				}
588			} else {
589				t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = float64(messageCount) / t.windowSize.Minutes()
590			}
591		} else {
592			t.metrics.MessagesPerMinute = 0
593		}
594	}
595	
596	// GetMetrics returns current computed metrics
597	func (t *TranscriptTailer) GetMetrics() *SessionMetrics {
598		if t.metrics == nil {
599			return &SessionMetrics{}
600		}
601		return t.metrics
602	}
603	
604	// ResetOffset resets the file offset and cumulative cost accumulators
605	// (useful for testing or file rotation).
606	func (t *TranscriptTailer) ResetOffset() {
607		t.lastOffset = 0
608		t.cumInputTokens = 0
609		t.cumOutputTokens = 0
610		t.cumCacheReadTokens = 0
611		t.cumCacheCreationTokens = 0
612		t.lastRequestID = ""
613		t.pendingSnapshot = nil
614	}
615	
616	// computeContextUtilization calculates context utilization percentage and pressure level.
617	func (t *TranscriptTailer) computeContextUtilization() {
618		if t.metrics.TotalTokens == 0 || t.metrics.ModelName == "" {
619			t.metrics.ContextUtilization = 0.0
620			t.metrics.PressureLevel = "unknown"
621			return
622		}
623	
624		var effectiveContextWindow int64
625	
626		if t.contextWindowOverride > 0 {
627			effectiveContextWindow = t.contextWindowOverride
628		}
629	
630		if effectiveContextWindow <= 0 && t.capacityMgr != nil {
631			cap := t.capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity(t.metrics.ModelName)
632			if ctx1m, ok := cap.BetaFeatures["context_1m"]; ok && ctx1m > 0 {
633				effectiveContextWindow = ctx1m
634			} else if cap.ContextWindow > 0 {
635				effectiveContextWindow = cap.ContextWindow
636			}
637		}
638	
639		// Unknown model: no context window data available — report raw tokens only.
640		if effectiveContextWindow <= 0 {
641			t.metrics.ContextWindow = 0
642			t.metrics.ContextUtilization = 0
643			t.metrics.PressureLevel = "unknown"
644			return
645		}
646	
647		utilizationPercentage := (float64(t.metrics.TotalTokens) / float64(effectiveContextWindow)) * 100
648	
649		pressureLevel := "safe"
650		if utilizationPercentage >= 90 {
651			pressureLevel = "critical"
652		} else if utilizationPercentage >= 80 {
653			pressureLevel = "warning"
654		} else if utilizationPercentage >= 60 {
655			pressureLevel = "caution"
656		}
657	
658		t.metrics.ContextWindow = effectiveContextWindow
659		t.metrics.ContextUtilization = utilizationPercentage
660		t.metrics.PressureLevel = pressureLevel
661	}
662	
663	// --- Model config fallback ---
664	
665	// getDefaultModelFromConfig reads the default model from the appropriate config
666	// based on adapter name.
667	func getDefaultModelFromConfig(adapter string) string {
668		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
669		if err != nil {
670			return ""
671		}
672		switch adapter {
673		case "pi":
674			return getPiModel(homeDir)
675		case "codex":
676			return getCodexModel(homeDir)
677		default:
678			return getClaudeModel(homeDir)
679		}
680	}
681	
682	func getClaudeModel(homeDir string) string {
683		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "settings.json"))
684		if err != nil {
685			return ""
686		}
687		var settings map[string]interface{}
688		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &settings); err != nil {
689			return ""
690		}
691		if model, ok := settings["model"].(string); ok {
692			return NormalizeModelName(model)
693		}
694		return ""
695	}
696	
697	func getCodexModel(homeDir string) string {
698		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".codex", "config.toml"))
699		if err != nil {
700			return ""
701		}
702		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
703			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
704			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "model") && strings.Contains(line, "=") {
705				parts := strings.SplitN(line, "=", 2)
706				if strings.TrimSpace(parts[0]) == "model" {
707					model := strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(parts[1]), "\"'")
708					if model != "" {
709						return model
710					}
711				}
712			}
713		}
714		return ""
715	}
716	
717	func getPiModel(homeDir string) string {
718		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".pi", "agent", "settings.json"))
719		if err != nil {
720			return ""
721		}
722		var settings map[string]interface{}
723		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &settings); err != nil {
724			return ""
725		}
726		if model, ok := settings["defaultModel"].(string); ok {
727			return NormalizeModelName(model)
728		}
729		return ""
730	}
